skill.name: agents-v2-py
skill.description: Build container-based Foundry Agents with Azure AI Projects SDK (ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition). Use when creating hosted agents with custom container images in Azure AI Foundry.
skill.summary: Create and manage container-based hosted agents in Azure AI Foundry using the Azure AI Projects SDK with custom Docker images and MCP tool integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a Python SDK documentation guide for Azure AI Foundry hosted agents. All 79 static analysis findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The markdown backticks were incorrectly flagged as shell execution, environment variable usage follows security best practices, and documentation URLs are not active network calls. No malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for Configuration
low_findings.description: The skill uses os.environ to read AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT. This is legitimate configuration access following security best practices (avoiding hardcoded secrets). Low risk as it only reads expected environment configuration.

content.user_title: Build Container-Based Azure AI Hosted Agents
content.value_statement: Deploy custom AI agents as containerized services in Azure AI Foundry without managing infrastructure. Use the Azure AI Projects SDK to define, deploy, and manage hosted agents with your own Docker images and integrated tools.
actual_capabilities: Create container-based hosted agents using ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition
Configure CPU and memory allocation for agent containers
Integrate code interpreter, file search, and MCP tools
Manage agent versions with list and delete operations
Pass environment variables securely to agent containers
Implement async patterns for agent creation
limitations: Requires Azure AI Project endpoint and proper authentication credentials
Container images must be pushed to Azure Container Registry (ACR) with proper pull permissions
Requires account-level capability host with enablePublicHostingEnvironment configured
Only supports AgentProtocol.RESPONSES protocol version v1

use_cases.title: Deploy Custom Data Processing Agent
use_cases.description: Create a hosted agent with a custom container that processes files using code interpreter and file search tools for automated data pipelines.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers building automated ETL workflows

use_cases.title: Build MCP-Enabled AI Assistant
use_cases.description: Deploy an agent that integrates external tools via MCP protocol, enabling seamless connection to custom APIs and services.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers extending AI capabilities with custom integrations

use_cases.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration System
use_cases.description: Create and manage multiple specialized hosted agents with different resource allocations and tool configurations for complex workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Solutions architects designing enterprise AI systems

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hosted agent using ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition with my container image at myregistry.azurecr.io/my-agent:v1, allocating 1 CPU and 2Gi memory with code interpreter enabled.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Deploy your first container-based agent

prompt_templates.title: Agent with Environment Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hosted agent that passes AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT and MODEL_NAME as environment variables to the container, using gpt-4o-mini as the model.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Configure agent behavior through environment variables

prompt_templates.title: MCP Tool Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy a hosted agent with both code interpreter and MCP tools. The MCP server is at https://my-mcp-server.example.com with label 'custom-tools'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Integrate external tools via MCP protocol

prompt_templates.title: Async Agent Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an async Python function that creates a hosted agent, lists all agent versions, and deletes outdated versions older than v2.0.

output_examples.input: Create a hosted agent with 2 CPU cores, 4Gi memory, and file search tool
output_examples.output:
Created hosted agent: data-processor-agent
Version: v1.0.0
State: Active
Resource allocation: 2 CPU, 4Gi memory
Tools enabled: code_interpreter, file_search

Version: v1.0.0, State: Active, Created: 2024-01-15
Version: v1.1.0, State: Active, Created: 2024-01-20
Version: v2.0.0, State: Active, Created: 2024-01-25

best_practices: Use specific image tags instead of 'latest' for production deployments to ensure reproducibility
Start with minimum resource allocation (1 CPU, 2Gi) and scale up based on actual usage patterns
Store all configuration in environment variables and use Azure Key Vault for sensitive values
anti_patterns: Hardcoding secrets or connection strings directly in the agent definition code
Using the 'latest' image tag in production which can cause unexpected behavior after image updates
Allocating maximum resources (4 CPU, 8Gi) without profiling actual resource requirements

faq.question: What is the minimum SDK version required for hosted agents?
faq.answer: Azure AI Projects SDK version 2.0.0b3 or later is required for ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition support.

faq.question: How do I grant my agent permission to pull from ACR?
faq.answer: Grant the Azure AI Project's managed identity the 'AcrPull' role assignment on your Azure Container Registry resource.

faq.question: What protocols do hosted agents support?
faq.answer: Hosted agents currently support AgentProtocol.RESPONSES with version 'v1' for standard agent interactions.

faq.question: Can I use multiple tools in a single hosted agent?
faq.answer: Yes, you can combine code_interpreter, file_search, and MCP tools in a single agent definition.

faq.question: How do I handle errors during agent creation?
faq.answer: Wrap agent creation in try/except blocks and check for common errors like ImagePullBackOff, InvalidContainerImage, and CapabilityHostNotFound.

faq.question: Should I delete old agent versions?
faq.answer: Yes, delete unused agent versions to free resources. Use list_versions to identify outdated versions before deletion.
